title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: members, contributors, leaders pledge make participation community harassment-free experience everyone, regardless age, body size, visible invisible disability, ethnicity, sex characteristics, gender identity expression, level experience, education, socio-economic status, nationality, personal appearance, race, caste, color, religion, sexual identity orientation. pledge act interact ways contribute open, welcoming, diverse, inclusive, healthy community.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Examples behavior contributes positive environment community include: Demonstrating empathy kindness toward people respectful differing opinions, viewpoints, experiences Giving gracefully accepting constructive feedback Accepting responsibility apologizing affected mistakes, learning experience Focusing best just us individuals, overall community Examples unacceptable behavior include: use sexualized language imagery, sexual attention advances kind Trolling, insulting derogatory comments, personal political attacks Public private harassment Publishing others’ private information, physical email address, without explicit permission conduct reasonably considered inappropriate professional setting

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community leaders responsible clarifying enforcing standards acceptable behavior take appropriate fair corrective action response behavior deem inappropriate, threatening, offensive, harmful. Community leaders right responsibility remove, edit, reject comments, commits, code, wiki edits, issues, contributions aligned Code Conduct, communicate reasons moderation decisions appropriate.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Code Conduct applies within community spaces, also applies individual officially representing community public spaces. Examples representing community include using official e-mail address, posting via official social media account, acting appointed representative online offline event.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Instances abusive, harassing, otherwise unacceptable behavior may reported community leaders responsible enforcement adam.sparks@curtin.edu.au. complaints reviewed investigated promptly fairly. community leaders obligated respect privacy security reporter incident.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community leaders follow Community Impact Guidelines determining consequences action deem violation Code Conduct:

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: Use inappropriate language behavior deemed unprofessional unwelcome community. Consequence: private, written warning community leaders, providing clarity around nature violation explanation behavior inappropriate. public apology may requested.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: violation single incident series actions. Consequence: warning consequences continued behavior. interaction people involved, including unsolicited interaction enforcing Code Conduct, specified period time. includes avoiding interactions community spaces well external channels like social media. Violating terms may lead temporary permanent ban.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: serious violation community standards, including sustained inappropriate behavior. Consequence: temporary ban sort interaction public communication community specified period time. public private interaction people involved, including unsolicited interaction enforcing Code Conduct, allowed period. Violating terms may lead permanent ban.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: Demonstrating pattern violation community standards, including sustained inappropriate behavior, harassment individual, aggression toward disparagement classes individuals. Consequence: permanent ban sort public interaction within community.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Code Conduct adapted Contributor Covenant, version 2.1, available https://www.contributor-covenant.org/version/2/1/code_of_conduct.html. Community Impact Guidelines inspired [Mozilla’s code conduct enforcement ladder][https://github.com/mozilla/inclusion]. answers common questions code conduct, see FAQ https://www.contributor-covenant.org/faq. Translations available https://www.contributor-covenant.org/translations.

title: Contributing to AAGISurvey
text: want make bigger change, ’s good idea first file issue make sure someone team agrees ’s needed. ’ve found bug, please file issue illustrates bug minimal reprex (also help write unit test, needed). See guide create great issue advice.

title: Contributing to AAGISurvey
text: Fork package clone onto computer. haven’t done , recommend using usethis::create_from_github("AAGI-AUS/CBADASReml", fork = TRUE). Install development dependencies devtools::install_dev_deps(), make sure package passes R CMD check running devtools::check(). R CMD check doesn’t pass cleanly, ’s good idea ask help continuing. Create Git branch pull request (PR). recommend using usethis::pr_init("brief-description--change"). Make changes, commit git, create PR running usethis::pr_push(), following prompts browser. title PR briefly describe change. body PR contain Fixes #issue-number. user-facing changes, add bullet top NEWS.md (.e. just first header). Follow style described https://style.tidyverse.org/news.html.

title: Contributing to AAGISurvey
text: use air formatting code style. New code follow formatting guidelines found air.toml. use roxygen2, Markdown syntax, documentation. use testthat unit tests. Contributions test cases included easier accept.

title: Contributing to AAGISurvey
text: Please note {CBADASReml} project released Contributor Code Conduct. contributing project agree abide terms.

title: Customising and Distributing the AAGI Collaborator Survey
text: Every experimental design data analysis output delivered part Service & Support collaboration accompanied opportunity collaborators provide feedback via AAGI Collaborator Survey. ensure relevant questions shown key contextual information captured alongside responses, survey link must customised sharing - means selecting details specific output recipient, type support provided, AAGI node, collaborator’s organisation type, encoded directly link. create_survey_url() function makes straightforward. Run per output delivery, generate correct link, print summary selections verify, copy URL clipboard ready paste delivery email.

title: Customising and Distributing the AAGI Collaborator Survey
text: install {AAGISurvey} package containing create_survey_url() function, first need enable AAGI R Universe R session. need . already enabled AAGI R Universe another package, can skip steps 1 & 2 jump straight Step 3. Load package. installed, need library(AAGISurvey) future sessions. create_survey_url() can run one two ways: Scripted – provide required arguments directly R script. Interactive – run function arguments follow prompts console. Scripted example: Interactive example approaches : Generate Qualtrics survey URL containing correct metadata Print summary selections Copy URL automatically clipboard, ready easy pasting email message. Check printed summary carefully confirm details match recipient output. Accessing Full Function Documentation detailed information function arguments, including valid coded values scripted example, can view full documentation R: Include survey link email delivers output. Research consistently shows survey response rates data quality meaningfully affected survey distributed (Dillman et al. 2014). Sending survey alongside output rather separate follow-addresses several factors directly: email comes someone recipient already working relationship , research shows meaningfully increases response rates (Ericson 2023; Millar et al. 2021); request contextually tied specific piece work rather arriving generic follow-; collaborators can respond experience fresh without asked recall details later. Reducing number separate communications also lowers friction well-documented contributor survey non-response (Dillman et al. 2014). adding link email, make sure : hyperLink customised survey URL “Go survey” button. Include full survey URL plain text button , button render correctly recipient’s email client, can copied pasted directly browser. Note Feedback collaborators helps us identify patterns, differences, emerging insights across contexts groups work - informing prioritise adapt work time remain relevant responsive needs. make providing feedback easy, email includes link short, anonymous survey output collaboration experience. Completing survey takes less five minutes.  (Alternatively, copy paste URL browser: http://YOUR_CUSTOMISED_SURVEY_URL.com) Thank taking time share thoughts. sending email, save copy ‘Correspondence’ folder associated collaboration project. ensures record maintained future reference accountability.
code: # example syntax for enabling the AAGI R-Universe options(   repos = c(     AAGI = "https://aagi-aus.r-universe.dev",     CRAN = "https://cloud.r-project.org"   ) ) install.packages("AAGISurvey") library(AAGISurvey) library(AAGISurvey)  # Create a survey URL for design support for small plot # trial for a government agency carried out by AAGI Cu  url <- create_survey_url(   support_type = "S_D",   design_type = "D_SP",   analysis_type = ,   aagi_node = "CU",   organisation_type = "O_GRO" ) library(AAGISurvey)  url <- create_survey_url() ?create_survey_url  # or  help("create_survey_url")

title: Customising and Distributing the AAGI Collaborator Survey
text: AAGI Collaborator Survey designed help us better understand impact Service & Support activities within Australian grains research, development extension (RD&E) sector. targets researchers work directly Service & Support collaborations, gathering structured feedback experimental design data analysis outputs collaboration experience surrounding . goal understand support effective, identify opportunities strengthen work collaborators time. survey designed data quality primary consideration, though quality means kind data differs important ways defined objective directly measured quantities, worth briefly unpacking. Surveys collect information participants’ responses, means capture people report, recall, perceive, judge. information inherently shaped individual experience, interpretation, context. weakness. defining characteristic data surveys built capture, one primary reasons surveys exist: provide efficient, scalable way access information measured directly instruments. Things like experiences, perceptions, judgements, interpretations. kind data, quality defined statistical precision replicability instrumental sense. defined well responses reflect participants’ genuine views experiences given questions asked context answered. Understanding shapes every design decision. Minimising objective burden (measurable effort imposed survey’s length, structure, demands) baseline obligation good research practice. Asking people invest time effort unnecessarily poor design , fundamentally, poor ethics. research suggests perceived burden, rather objective burden, decisive determinant survey response (data) quality (Kunz Gummer 2024). Perceived burden reflects respondent’s subjective experience survey, difficult, effortful, worthwhile find , consistently predicts response quality across range indicators including item completion, attention, response depth (Kunz Gummer 2024). Perceived burden determined effort alone. reflects relationship perceived effort perceived value, research consistently identifies motivation, trust, topic salience interacting factors shape whether people participate, complete survey, invest quality responses (Krosnick 1991; Groves et al. 2000). survey feels relevant, comes organisation perceived credible responsive, participation feels like amount something, experienced less burdensome regardless length. Critically, perceived burden shaped largely respondent encounters survey , prior experience, reputation, whether past participation felt worthwhile (Bradburn 1978; Krosnick 1991). means levers managing extend well beyond survey design operate organisation time. data matters much collect . Failing act responses missed opportunity, ethical breach implicit agreement research participation represents. communities work grains sector, networks tight reputations travel, consequences extend beyond single survey shape future participation, future researchers, received. interested, principles behavioural methodological evidence behind explored depth Behaviourally-Informed Survey Design: Methodological Guide. value survey depends much data collected, worth thinking beyond distribution. findings shared collaborators, whether can see input reflected changes communications follow, directly affects worthwhile participating feels extension quality consistency responses time. evolving process room develop collectively. ideas findings might acted , insights communicated back collaborators meaningful ways, suggestions improve structure, wording, workflow survey , please share emailing CBADA@curtin.edu.au subject line AAGI Collaborator Survey.

what: Create an AAGI Survey URL — create_survey_url

text: function designed used time need generate survey URL requesting feedback partner. embeds information already known team respondents need provide . includes type support provided, output delivered, AAGI node delivered work, organisation type partner. details included metadata within URL survey platform can automatically link respondents feedback relevant contextual information.

title: Create an AAGI Survey URL — create_survey_url
code: create_survey_url(   support_type = NULL,   design_type = NULL,   analysis_type = NULL,   aagi_node = NULL,   organisation_type = NULL )

title: Create an AAGI Survey URL — create_survey_url
text: support_type Character vector specifying type(s) support evaluated. may choose one . Options : "S_D" Experimental design support "S_A" Analysis support design_type Character string specifying type experimental design (required "S_D" selected support_type). Options : "D_SP" Small plot trial design "D_OFE" farm experiment design "D_GH" Glasshouse experiment design "D_GC" Growth chamber experiment design "D_LAB" Lab experiment design "D_OTHER" design type chosen recipient analysis_type Character string analysis type. Required "S_A" selected support_type. Options : "A_SP" Small plot trial data analysis "A_OFE" farm experiment data analysis "A_PRO" Protected environment experiment data analysis (glasshouse growth chamber) "A_BIO" Bioinformatics genetic data "A_BRE" Breeding selection program data "A_ENV" Environmental geospatial data "A_IMG" Imagery data "A_REC" Farm records monitoring data "A_OTHER" type selected recipient aagi_node Character string AAGI node. Options : "CU" Curtin University "UA" University Adelaide "UQ" University Queensland organisation_type Character string organisation type. Options : "O_GRO" Grower group, industry association farm cooperative "O_AGR" Agronomy farm advisory practice "O_ACA" Academic institution "O_GOV" Government agency department "O_BRE" Seed breeding company "O_TEC" Technology, biotechnology chemical company "O_OTHER" organisation type chosen recipient

title: Create an AAGI Survey URL — create_survey_url
code: if (FALSE) { # interactive() # create a survey URL for # - a small plot trial design & analysis # - for a government agency or department # - performed by CU create_survey_url(   support_type = c("S_D", "S_A"),   design_type = "D_SP",   analysis_type = "A_SP",   aagi_node = "CU",   organisation_type = "O_GOV" )  # create a survey URL for # - a bioinformatics analysis # - for an academic institution # - performed by UA create_survey_url(   support_type = "S_A",   analysis_type = "A_BIO",   aagi_node = "UA",   organisation_type = "O_ACA" ) }
